Please note that the long stay (Exchange Visa) Visa application must be submitted at the Visa Application Centre in Tehran. Please click here (http://www.southafricanembassy.ir/embassy/visa-services/) for more information.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Please note that the long stay (Exchange Visa) Visa application must be submitted at the Visa Application Centre in Tehran. Please click here (http://www.southafricanembassy.ir/embassy/visa-services/) for more information.

## Visa Fees
Kindly click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1nGWACJcVTsPp8pMkPaXSe/75e998fa9d8a5fface39c9a86b51a898/Exchange-Visa-recent.pdf) for Visa Application Fees 
- Applicant shall be charged a service charge of Euro 57 per application in addition to the visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable
- Applicants are requested to carry exact change in Euros to pay the fees for services used at the Visa Application Centre.

## Documents Required
For information on the following: 
 Medical Report click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3hXmMtRMsXjcQqUz6M8N9w/c09bf91bbf3235e010f2a839779087aa/Medical-Report-Recent.pdf)
 South Africa's Long Stay Visa Requirements click here 

Please note: 
- Non-Iranian nationals can apply for a South African visa if they have a long-term Iranian residence permit in their passport.

## Processing Time
Applicants should take into consideration that the process of examining a fully completed application will take 30 - 60 working days.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

## Download Forms
Long Stay Application Form click here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/48P1poXG3HBU2dNfHmrC91/73b0cbfff1a569fbaddaff223a9ce042/Long-Stay-Application-Form.pdf)

visa_types.name: Business
visa_types.documents_required:
- Visa application form
- Completed Visa application Form (BI84) Form 11 in
 
BLACK ink only.
- Two recent colored photograph
- Must be a clear copy, not older than 6 months, size 3.5 cm × 4.5 cm, white background
- Passport
- Must be valid for no less than 3 months after the expiry of intended visit to South Africa and containing at least 2 blank visa pages (one to endorse/stamp the visa and the other one for entry/exit stamps.
- Passport copies of page 2 and 3 & previous South Africa visa copies and copy of Bio Pages of previous passport (included South Africa Visa) (all copies should be in A4 format)

(Proof of Employment) No-objection letter from employer or sponsor (Original)
- It must be signed and stamped; dated/issued on the current month of should be dated not more than a month from the date of application.
- The letter must confirm the applicant’s name , passport number, current job position, amount of salary, start of work date , travel dates, exact purpose of travel and “no objection” to leave of absence
from the sponsor/employer for the said travel. Full contact details of the company/employer (e.g. physical address, telephone number, name,and Job title) must be indicated in the letter.
- For self-sponsored, Officially translated valid trade license.
- For Doctors:valid medical license officially translated.
- For lawyers:valid lawyer license officially translated.
Unaccompanied Minor / Travelling with one Parent
- “AFFIDAVIT FORM”
 
must be filled in
 
BLACK INK ONLY
 
by
 
BOTH
 
parents/guardian and must be signed before the
 
Commissioner of Oath
. A copy of parent’s passport should also be submitted
- Official consent letter from parent that is not travelling (with dates and destination matching those of the application)
- Proof of guardianship or custody – accompanied by person other than parent
- Officially translated Birth certificate (contains MFA & justice stamps)
- Proof of adoption – adopted minor

For applicants MARRIED to a SOUTH AFRICAN, the following additional documents are required:
- Copy of Marriage certificate
- Copy of South African ID or South African passport of the spouse (Certified by the Police Authority)
For Business/Conference/Trade fair Applicants
- Letter from both Iran and South African host company
- Both letters must confirm the
 
applicant’s name, passport number, travel dates, exact purpose of the travel
 
and
 
financial undertaking
.
- Both letters must be signed (with the signatory name and designation) and stamped. Also, it must be dated/issued on the current month or not more than a month, from the date of application.
- Signatory for the South African Company need to provide the South African ID copy certified by the police Authority
- Both letters must have the company’s full contact details (e.g. physical address and telephone numbers).
- If the financial undertaking is not indicated in the letters, the applicant must submit his/her own latest three (3) months personal bank transaction (stamped by the bank).For self-sponsored, valid trade license copy

- Ticket
- Proof of two way Air tickets (provisional). Proof of Domestic flight/s is also required, if the applicant is travelling from one city to another.

- Accommodation
- If staying in a hotel, a proof of confirmed or provisional booking is required
- If staying with a family/relative/friend/colleague, a copy of the following from the SA host are required
- Invitation letter
 
(typed, signed and must be dated/issued on the current month or should be dated not more than a month from the date of application). Bio information of guest should be mentioned in invitation letter , dates ,duration of stay , the physical home address and contact details of the SA host should be indicated in the letter.
- Proof of address (e.g. Utility bills, Lease of Contract, Tax invoice, etc.) as indicated in the invitation letter.
- If the host is a South African, a copy of South African ID book or passport is required. If the host is not a South African, a copy of passport and residence/permit/visa copy of the host in South Africa is required ( All Documents should be certified by Police Authority)
- For parents and siblings visiting either their son/daughter/siblings in South Africa, and will be staying with them whilst in South Africa, the following additional documents are required:
- Copy of son/daughter/sibling’s full birth certificate (In English only)
- Copy of son/daughter/sibling’s passport (Certified by the Police Authority )
- Copy of visa/temporary residence/permanent residence permit issued/granted to them (Certified by the police Authority)

- Proof of financial means.
- Applicant’s latest three (3) months banks statement(Transaction)(in A4 format).
 
ONLY original Stamped bank
 
satatement(Transaction) issued not more than 10days by a bank (English Only) are acceptable.
- if the applicant submit the bank satatement(Transaction) of the company, the following additional documents are required:
- Letter from the company/sponsor which states that the company has no objection to use the bank account/satatement(Transaction) by the applicant, to cover his/her travel expenses in South Africa. The letter must be signed by ALL partners/owners of the company (as shown in the trade license).
- Copy of Trade License Or Gazette notice translated in to English
- 3 months latest bank satatement(Transaction) of the company (stamped by the bank in A4 format)

Please note:
- Non-Iranian nationals can apply for a South African visa if they have a long-term Iranian residence permit in their passport.
- All visa applicants, who are applying for the first time, must only submit the application a MONTH or closer to the date of intended travel to South Africa.
- All visa/permit applications must be applied “In-person” by the applicant ONLY.
- It takes 10 working days to process the visa.
- Original passport cannot be retrieved until application has been processed.
- Consular section will only accept / process complete visa applications – it is the responsibility of the applicant to ensure that he or she submits a complete application.
- Cabin Crew and Pilot can withdraw their passports 2 WORKING DAYS after the application submission.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Business Visa is required if during any portion of the visit applicant intends to conduct business or commercial activities. E.g.; Meetings, Conference, Training Visas, Trade Fairs,
                                                                            etc; such visas can only be issued to travelers not being paid for work by an entity within South Africa. If the applicant intends to undertake paid work in South Africa he/she
                                                                            will need to obtain a work permit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Business Visa is required if during any portion of the visit applicant intends to conduct business or commercial activities. E.g.; Meetings, Conference, Training Visas, Trade Fairs,
                                                                            etc; such visas can only be issued to travelers not being paid for work by an entity within South Africa. If the applicant intends to undertake paid work in South Africa he/she
                                                                            will need to obtain a work permit.

## SOME IMPORTANT POINTS
Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render the application incomplete:
We strongly recommend that you pay careful attention to the Overview, Documents Required and Processing Time sections of your chosen visa category, before proceeding with your application.
Please be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that the
                                                                            South Africa Embassy will grant your request.
Please be aware that applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum
                                                                            as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
Visa Fee / VFS Logistic Charges can be accepted in Cash Only.
Documents issued in a Regional language must be officially translated to English.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Business]

 Please note: 
- The applicable visa fee in Iranian Rial is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be charged a service fee of Euro 57 per application in addition to the visa fees and must be paid in cash.
- All fees are non-refundable.
- Applicants are requested to carry exact change in Euros to pay the fees for services used at the Visa Application Centre.

## Photo Specifications
 Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications 
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation.
                                                                            Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs 
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger
                                                                                than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General
                                                                                may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the
                                                                                person is blind
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner
Please refer Photo specification guide (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2IclBw38KZwQVjy2ocWbbh/6f49b8129c7de56eea9802bfbd2c6873/photo_specification.pdf)

## Processing Time
 Applicants should take into consideration that the process of examining a fully completed application is 10 working days..
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

## Application Form
Please download and complete fully the application form below, ensure it is signed and please bring with you when attending for your appointment. 
Visa Application form – (BI84) Form 11 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3cgxyx7zO6y0TUvTpBHQng/d1c33011fbddbd98146eac0485c74170/APPLICATION-FOR-VISA-OR-TRANSIT-VISA.pdf)
Download Instructions:
To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

## Overview
 Medical Treatment visas may be issued for a maximum period of six months at a time
Payment of the prescribed fee
A letter from your medical practitioner or a medical institution, indicating the reasons/ necessity for treatment, the period of treatment and particulars of the treatment plans in
                                                                            the Republic of South Africa

## SOME IMPORTANT POINTS
Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render the application incomplete:
We strongly recommend that you pay careful attention to the Overview, Documents Required and Processing Time sections of your chosen visa category, before proceeding with your application.
Please be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that the
                                                                            South Africa Embassy will grant your request.
Please be aware that applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum
                                                                            as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
Visa Fee / VFS Logistic Charges can be accepted in Cash Only.
Documents issued in a Regional language must be officially translated to English.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Medical Visit]

Please note: 
- The applicable visa fee in Iranian Rial is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be charged a service fee of Euro 57 per application in addition to the visa fees and must be paid in cash.
- All fees are non-refundable.
- Applicants are requested to carry exact change in Euros to pay the fees for services used at the Visa Application Centre.

## Documents Required
- Visa application form
- Completed Visa application Form (BI84) Form 11 in
 
BLACK ink only.
- Two recent colored photograph
- Must be a clear copy, not older than 6 months, size 3.5 cm × 4.5 cm, white background
- Passport
- Must be valid for no less than 3 months after the expiry of intended visit to South Africa and containing at least 2 blank visa pages (one to endorse/stamp the visa and the other one for entry/exit stamps.
- Passport copies of page 2 and 3 & previous South Africa visa copies and coy of bio pages of previous passport(included South Africa visa(all copies should be in A4 format)
- (Proof of Employment) No-objection letter from employer or sponsor (Original)
- It must be signed and stamped; dated/issued on the current month of should be dated not more than a month from the date of application.
- The letter must confirm the applicant’s name,passport number, current job position, amount of salary, start of work date, travel dates, exact purpose of travel and “no objection” to leave of absence
from the sponsor/employer for the said travel. Full contact details of the company/employer (e.g. physical address, telephone number,name,and Job title) must be indicated in the letter.
- For self-sponsored,Oficially translated valid trade license
- For Doctors:valid medical License officially translated.
- For Lawyers :valid lawyer License officially translated.
Unaccompanied Minor / Travelling with one Parent
- “AFFIDAVIT FORM”
 
must be filled in
 
BLACK INK ONLY
 
by
 
BOTH
 
parents/guardian and must be signed before the
 
Commissioner of Oath
. A copy of parent’s passport should also be submitted
- Official consent from parent that is not travelling(with dates and destination matching those of the application)
- Proof of guardianship or custody – accompanied by person other than parent
- Officially translated Birth certificate(contains MFA & justice stamps)
- Proof of adoption – adopted minor
For applicants MARRIED to a SOUTH AFRICAN, the following additional documents are required:
- Copy of Marriage certificate
- Copy of South African ID or South African passport of the spouse (Certified by the Police Authority)
- Ticket
- Proof of two way Air tickets (provisional). Proof of Domestic flight/s is also required, if the applicant is travelling from one city to another.
- Accommodation
- If staying in a hotel, a proof of provisional booking is required
- If staying with a family/relative/friend/colleague, a copy of the following from the SA host are required
- Invitation letter
 
(typed, signed and must be dated/issued on the current month or should be dated not more than a month from the date of application). Bio information of Guest shoulb be mentioned in invitation Letter, dates ,duration of stay , the physical home address and contact details of the SA host should be indicated in the letter.
- Proof of address (e.g. Utility bills, Lease of Contract, Tax invoice, etc.) as indicated in the invitation letter.
- If the host is a South African, a copy of South African ID book or passport is required. If the host is not a South African, a copy of passport and residence/permit/visa copy of the host in South Africa is required ( All Documents should be certified by Police Authority)
- For parents and siblings visiting either their son/daughter/siblings in South Africa, and will be staying with them whilst in South Africa, the following additional documents are required:
- Copy of son/daughter/sibling’s full birth certificate (In English only)
- Copy of son/daughter/sibling’s passport (Certified by the Police Authority )
- Copy of visa/temporary residence/permanent residence permit issued/granted to them (Certified by the police Authority)
- Proof of financial means.
- Applicant’s latest three (3) months banks statement(transaction)(in A4 format).
 
ONLY original stamped bank
 
statement(transaction) issued not more than 10days by a bank (English Only) are acceptable.
- Should the applicant submit the bank statement(transaction) of the company, the following additional documents are required:
- Letter from the company/sponsor which states that the company has no objection to use the bank account/statement(transaction) by the applicant, to cover his/her travel expenses in South Africa. The letter must be signed by ALL partners/owners of the company (as shown in the trade license).
- Copy of Trade License Or Gazette notice translated in to English
- 3 months latest bank statement(transaction) of the company (stamped by the bank in A4 format)

Please note:
- Non-Iranian nationals can apply for a South African visa if they have a long-term Iranian residence permit in their passport.
- All visa applicants, who are applying for the first time, must only submit the application a MONTH or closer to the date of intended travel to South Africa.
- All visa/permit applications must be applied “In-person” by the applicant ONLY.
- It takes 10 working days to process the visa.
- Original passport cannot be retrieved until application has been processed.
- Consular section will only accept / process complete visa applications – it is the responsibility of the applicant to ensure that he or she submits a complete application.
- Cabin Crew and Pilot can withdraw their passports 2 WORKING DAYS after the application submission.

## Photo Specifications
 Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications 
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation.
                                                                            Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs 
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger
                                                                                than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General
                                                                                may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the
                                                                                person is blind
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner
Please refer Photo specification guide (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2IclBw38KZwQVjy2ocWbbh/6f49b8129c7de56eea9802bfbd2c6873/photo_specification.pdf)

## Processing Time
 Applicants should take into consideration that the process of examining a fully completed application is 10 working days..
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

## Application Form
Please download and complete fully the application form below, ensure it is signed and please bring with you when attending for your appointment. 
Visa Application form – (BI84) Form 11 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3cgxyx7zO6y0TUvTpBHQng/d1c33011fbddbd98146eac0485c74170/APPLICATION-FOR-VISA-OR-TRANSIT-VISA.pdf)
Download Instructions:
To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

- Proof of two way Air tickets (provisional or confirmed). Proof of Domestic flight/s is also required, if the applicant is travelling from one city to another.
- Accommodation
- If staying in a hotel, a proof of confirmed or provisional booking is required
- If staying with a family/relative/friend/colleague, a copy of the following from the SA host are required
- Invitation letter
 
(typed, signed and must be dated/issued on the current month or should be dated not more than a month from the date of application).Bio information of guest should be mentioned in invitation letter , The physical home address and contact details of the SA host should be indicated in the letter.
- Proof of address (e.g. Utility bills, Lease of Contract, Tax invoice, etc.) as indicated in the invitation letter.
- If the host is a South African, a copy of South African ID book or passport is required. If the host is not a South African, a copy of passport and residence/permit/visa copy of the host in South Africa is required ( All Documents should be certified by Police Authority)
- For parents and siblings visiting either their son/daughter/siblings in South Africa, and will be staying with them whilst in South Africa, the following additional documents are required:
- Copy of son/daughter/sibling’s full birth certificate (In English only)
- Copy of son/daughter/sibling’s passport (Certified by the Police Authority )
- Copy of visa/temporary residence/permanent residence permit issued/granted to them (Certified by the police Authority)
- Proof of financial means.
- Applicant’s latest three (3) months banks statement (transaction)(in A4 format).
 
ONLY original Stamped bank
 
statement(transaction) issued not more than 10days by a bank (English Only) are acceptable.
- if the applicant submit the bank statement(transaction) of the company, the following additional documents are required:

- Copy of Trade License Or Gazette notice translated in to English
- 3 months latest bank statement(transaction) of the company (stamped by the bank and in A4 format)

Please note:
- Non-Iranian nationals can apply for a South African visa if they have a long-term Iranian residence permit in their passport.
- All visa applicants, who are applying for the first time, must only submit the application a MONTH or closer to the date of intended travel to South Africa.
- All visa/permit applications must be applied “In-person” by the applicant ONLY.
- It takes 10 working days to process the visa.
- Original passport cannot be retrieved until application has been processed.
- Consular section will only accept / process complete visa applications – it is the responsibility of the applicant to ensure that he or she submits a complete application.
- Cabin Crew and Pilot can withdraw their passports 2 WORKING DAYS after the application submission.

## Photo Specifications
 Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications 
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation.
                                                                                Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs 
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not
                                                                                    larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The
                                                                                    Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if
                                                                                    the person is blind
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner

- It must be signed and stamped; dated/issued on the current month of should be dated not more than a month from the date of application.
- The letter must confirm the
 
applicant’s name, passport number, current job position, amount of salary, start of work date, travel dates, exact purpose of travel and “no objection” to leave of absence

from the sponsor/employer for the said travel. Full contact details of the company/employer (e.g. physical address, telephone number, name,and Job title) must be indicated in the letter.
- For self-sponsored, officially translated valid trade license.
- For Doctors:valid medical license officially translated.
- For lawyers:valid lawyer license officially translated.

Unaccompanied Minor / Travelling with one Parent
- “AFFIDAVIT FORM”
 
must be filled in
 
BLACK INK ONLY
 
by
 
BOTH
 
parents/guardian and must be signed before the
 
Commissioner of Oath
. A copy of parent’s passport should also be submitted
- Official consent letter from parent that is not travelling (with date and destination matching those of the application)
- Proof of guardianship or custody – accompanied by person other than parent
- Officially translated Birth certificate(contains MFA & justice stamps)
- Proof of adoption – adopted minor
For applicants MARRIED to a SOUTH AFRICAN, the following additional documents are required:
- Copy of Marriage certificate
- Copy of South African ID or South African passport of the spouse (Certified by the Police Authority)
- Ticket
- Proof of Tow way Air tickets (provisional). Proof of Domestic flight/s is also required, if the applicant is travelling from one city to another.
- Accommodation
- If staying in a hotel, a proof of provisional booking is required
- If staying with a family/relative/friend/colleague, a copy of the following from the SA host are required
- Invitation letter
 
(typed, signed and must be dated/issued on the current month or should be dated not more than a month from the date of application) Bio information of guest should be mentioned in invitation letter, Date ,duration of stay, The physical home address and contact details of the SA host should be indicated in the letter.
- Proof of address (e.g. Utility bills, Lease of Contract, Tax invoice, etc.) as indicated in the invitation letter.
- If the host is a South African, a copy of South African ID book or passport is required. If the host is not a South African, a copy of passport and residence/permit/visa copy of the host in South Africa is required ( All Documents should be certified by Police Authority)
- For parents and siblings visiting either their son/daughter/siblings in South Africa, and will be staying with them whilst in South Africa, the following additional documents are required:
- Copy of son/daughter/sibling’s full birth certificate (In English only)
- Copy of son/daughter/sibling’s passport (Certified by the Police Authority )
- Copy of visa/temporary residence/permanent residence permit issued/granted to them (Certified by the police Authority)
- Proof of financial means.
- Applicant’s latest three (3) months banks statement(transaction)in A4format. ONLY original Stamped bank statement(transaction) issued not more than 10days by a bank (English Only) are acceptable.
- if the applicant submit the bank statement(transaction) of the company, the following additional documents are required:
- Letter from the company/sponsor which states that the company has no objection to use the bank account/statement(transaction) by the applicant, to cover his/her travel expenses in South Africa. The letter must be signed by ALL partners/owners of the company (as shown in the trade license).
- Copy of Trade License Or Gazette notice translated in to English
- 3 months latest bank statement(transaction) of the company (stamped by the bank in A4 format)

Please note: 
- Non-Iranian nationals can apply for a South African visa if they have a long-term Iranian residence permit in their passport.
- All visa applicants, who are applying for the first time, must only submit the application a MONTH or closer to the date of intended travel to South Africa.
- All visa/permit applications must be applied “In-person” by the applicant ONLY.
- It takes 10 working days to process the visa.
- Original passport cannot be retrieved until application has been processed.
- Consular section will only accept / process complete visa applications – it is the responsibility of the applicant to ensure that he or she submits a complete application.
- Cabin Crew and Pilot can withdraw their passports 2 WORKING DAYS after the application submission.

We strongly recommend that you pay careful attention to the Overview, Documents Required and Processing Time sections of your chosen visa category, before proceeding with your application.
Please be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that the
                                                                            South Africa Embassy will grant your request.
Please be aware that applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum

Visa Fee / VFS Logistic Charges can be accepted in Cash Only.

## Photo Specifications
 Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications 
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation.
                                                                                Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs 
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not
                                                                                    larger than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The
                                                                                    Director-General may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if
                                                                                    the person is blind
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner
Please refer Photo specification guide (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2IclBw38KZwQVjy2ocWbbh/6f49b8129c7de56eea9802bfbd2c6873/photo_specification.pdf)

 Applicants should take into consideration that the process of examining a fully completed application is 10 working days..
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

## Application Form
Please download and complete fully the application form below, ensure it is signed and please bring with you when attending for your appointment. 
Visa Application form – (BI84) Form 11 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3cgxyx7zO6y0TUvTpBHQng/d1c33011fbddbd98146eac0485c74170/APPLICATION-FOR-VISA-OR-TRANSIT-VISA.pdf)
Download Instructions:
To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

- Visa application form
- Completed Visa application Form (BI84) Form 11 in
 
BLACK ink only.
- Two recent colored photograph
- Must be a clear copy, not older than 6 months, size 3.5 cm × 4.5 cm, white background
- Passport
- Must be valid for no less than 3 months after the expiry of intended visit to South Africa and containing at least 2 blank visa pages (one to endorse/stamp the visa and the other one for entry/exit stamps.
- Passport copies of page 2 and 3 & previous South Africa visa copies and copy of Bio Pages of previous passport (included South Africa Visa) (all copies should be in A4 format)
- (Proof of Employment ) No-objection letter from employer or sponsor (Original)
- It must be signed and stamped; dated/issued on the current month of should be dated not more than a month from the date of application.
- The letter must confirm the applicant’s name , passport number,current job position,amount of salary,start of work date, travel dates, exact purpose of travel and “no objection” to leave of absence
from the sponsor/employer for the said travel. Full contact details of the company/employer (e.g. physical address, telephone number,name, Job title) must be indicated in the letter.
- For self-sponsored, valid trade officially translated license.
- For Doctors, valid medical License officially translated.
- For Lawyer: valid lawyers license officially translated.
- Travel Itinerary(day by day travel plan)
Unaccompanied Minor / Travelling with one Parent
- “AFFIDAVIT FORM”
 
must be filled in
 
BLACK INK ONLY
 
by
 
BOTH
 
parents/guardian and must be signed before the
 
Commissioner of Oath
. A copy of parent’s passport should also be submitted
- Official consent letter from parent that is not travelling (with dates and destination matching those of application)
- Proof of guardianship or custody – accompanied by person other than parent
- Officially Translated Birth certificate(contains MFA & justice stamps)
- Proof of adoption – adopted minor
For applicants MARRIED to a SOUTH AFRICAN, the following additional documents are required:
- Copy of Marriage certificate
- Copy of South African ID or South African passport of the spouse (Certified by the Police Authority)
- Ticket
- Proof of two way Air tickets (provisional). Proof of Domestic flight/s is also required, if the applicant is travelling from one city to another.
- Accommodation
- If staying in a hotel, a proof of provisional booking is required
- If staying with a family/relative/friend/colleague, a copy of the following from the SA host are required
- Invitation letter (typed, signed and must be dated/issued on the current month or should be dated not more than a month from the date of application). Bio information of guest should be mentioned in invitation letter , Date, duration of stay, the physical home address and contact details of the SA host should be indicated in the letter.
- Proof of address (e.g. Utility bills, Lease of Contract, Tax invoice, etc.) as indicated in the invitation letter.
- If the host is a South African, a copy of South African ID book or passport is required. If the host is not a South African, a copy of passport and residence/permit/visa copy of the host in South Africa is required ( All Documents should be certified by Police Authority)
- For parents and siblings visiting either their son/daughter/siblings in South Africa, and will be staying with them whilst in South Africa, the following additional documents are required:
- Copy of son/daughter/sibling’s full birth certificate (In English only)
- Copy of son/daughter/sibling’s passport (Certified by the Police Authority )
- Copy of visa/temporary residence/permanent residence permit issued/granted to them (Certified by the police Authority)
- Proof of financial means.
- Applicant’s latest three (3) months banks statement(transaction)(in A4 format).
 
ONLY original Stamped bank
 
statement(transaction) issued not more than 10days by a bank (English Only) are acceptable.
- if the applicant submit the bank statement(transaction) of the company, the following additional documents are required:
- Letter from the company/sponsor which states that the company has no objection to use the bank account/statement(transaction) by the applicant, to cover his/her travel expenses in South Africa. The letter must be signed by ALL partners/owners of the company (as shown in the trade license).
- Copy of Trade License Or Gazette notice translated in to English
- 3 months latest bank statement(transaction) of the company (stamped by the bank,in A4 format)

Please note:
- Non-Iranian nationals can apply for a South African visa if they have a long-term Iranian residence permit in their passport.
- All visa applicants, who are applying for the first time, must only submit the application a MONTH or closer to the date of intended travel to South Africa.
- All visa/permit applications must be applied “In-person” by the applicant ONLY.
- It takes 10 working days to process the visa.
- Original passport cannot be retrieved until application has been processed.
- Consular section will only accept / process complete visa applications – it is the responsibility of the applicant to ensure that he or she submits a complete application.
- Cabin Crew and Pilot can withdraw their passports 2 WORKING DAYS after the application submission.

## Photo Specifications
 Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications 
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation.
                                                                            Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs 
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger
                                                                                than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General
                                                                                may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the
                                                                                person is blind
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner
Please refer Photo specification guide (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2IclBw38KZwQVjy2ocWbbh/6f49b8129c7de56eea9802bfbd2c6873/photo_specification.pdf)

## Processing Time
 Applicants should take into consideration that the process of examining a fully completed application is 10 working days..
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

## Application Form
Please download and complete fully the application form below, ensure it is signed and please bring with you when attending for your appointment. 
Visa Application form – (BI84) Form 11 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3cgxyx7zO6y0TUvTpBHQng/d1c33011fbddbd98146eac0485c74170/APPLICATION-FOR-VISA-OR-TRANSIT-VISA.pdf)
Download Instructions:
To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

- Both letters must be signed (with the signatory name and designation) and stamped. Also, it must be dated/issued on the current month or not more than a month, from the date of application.

- Accommodation
- If staying in a hotel, a proof of provisional booking is required
- If staying with a family/relative/friend/colleague, a copy of the following from the SA host are required
- Invitation letter
 
(typed, signed and must be dated/issued on the current month or should be dated not more than a month from the date of application). Bio information of guest should be mentioned in invitation letter Dates, duration of stay , the physical home address and contact details of the SA host should be indicated in the letter.

statements (transaction) issued not more than 10days by a bank (English Only) are acceptable.

Specifications 
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation.
                                                                            Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs 
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger
                                                                                than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General
                                                                                may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames

                                                                                person is blind
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.

from the sponsor/employer for the said travel. Full contact details of the company/employer (e.g. physical address, telephone number,Job title) must be indicated in the letter.
- For self-sponsored, Oficially translated valid trade license.
- For Doctors:valid Medical License Officially Transalated
- For Lawyers:Valid Lawyer License Officially Transalated
Unaccompanied Minor / Travelling with one Parent
- “AFFIDAVIT FORM”
 
must be filled in
 
BLACK INK ONLY
 
by
 
BOTH
 
parents/guardian and must be signed before the
 
Commissioner of Oath
. A copy of parent’s passport should also be submitted
- Official consent Letter from parent that is not travelling(with dates and destination matching those of application
- Proof of guardianship or custody – accompanied by person other than parent
- Officially translated Birth certificate(contains MFA & justice stamps)
- Proof of adoption – adopted minor
For applicants MARRIED to a SOUTH AFRICAN, the following additional documents are required:
- Copy of Marriage certificate
- Copy of South African ID or South African passport of the spouse (Certified by the Police Authority)
- Official invitation letter from University or Relevant institute
- Ticket
- Proof of two way Air tickets (provisional). Proof of Domestic flight/s is also required, if the applicant is travelling from one city to another.
- Accommodation
- If staying in a hotel, a proof of provisional booking is required
- If staying with a family/relative/friend/colleague, a copy of the following from the SA host are required
- Invitation letter
 
(typed, signed and must be dated/issued on the current month or should be dated not more than a month from the date of application). Bio information of guest should be mentioned in invitation letter ,The physical home address and contact details of the SA host should be indicated in the letter.
- Proof of address (e.g. Utility bills, Lease of Contract, Tax invoice, etc.) as indicated in the invitation letter.
- If the host is a South African, a copy of South African ID book or passport is required. If the host is not a South African, a copy of passport and residence/permit/visa copy of the host in South Africa is required ( All Documents should be certified by Police Authority)
- For parents and siblings visiting either their son/daughter/siblings in South Africa, and will be staying with them whilst in South Africa, the following additional documents are required:
- Copy of son/daughter/sibling’s full birth certificate (In English only)
- Copy of son/daughter/sibling’s passport (Certified by the Police Authority )
- Copy of visa/temporary residence/permanent residence permit issued/granted to them (Certified by the police Authority)
- Proof of financial means.
- Applicant’s latest three (3) months banks statement(transaction) in A4.
 
ONLY original Stamped bank
 
statement(transaction) issued not more than 10days by a bank (English Only) are acceptable.
- Should the applicant submit the bank statement(transaction) of the company, the following additional documents are required:
- Letter from the company/sponsor which states that the company has no objection to use the bank account/statement(transaction) by the applicant, to cover his/her travel expenses in South Africa. The letter must be signed by ALL partners/owners of the company (as shown in the trade license).
- Copy of Trade License Or Gazette notice translated in to English
- 3 months latest bank statement(transaction) of the company (stamped by the bank,in A4 format)

Please note:
- Non-Iranian nationals can apply for a South African visa if they have a long-term Iranian residence permit in their passport.
- All visa applicants, who are applying for the first time, must only submit the application a MONTH or closer to the date of intended travel to South Africa.
- All visa/permit applications must be applied “In-person” by the applicant ONLY.
- It takes 10 working days to process the visa.
- Original passport cannot be retrieved until application has been processed.
- Consular section will only accept / process complete visa applications – it is the responsibility of the applicant to ensure that he or she submits a complete application.
- Cabin Crew and Pilot can withdraw their passports 2 WORKING DAYS after the application submission.

## Photo Specifications
 Applicants are requested to submit their Application form with the new photo format, as explained below. 
Sample Photograph: Below specs to be maintained at 45mm x 35mm 
Specifications 
The successful delivery of some of the department's services relies not only on our efforts. You have the responsibility of providing the Department with the correct documentation.
                                                                            Please find details below for all supporting documentation that the department requires: 
Photographs 
Photographs submitted for identity documents and passports must comply to the following specifications: 
- Length - 45mm
- Width - 35mm
- The background of the photograph shall be plain and free of shadows and shall not have a white lining
- Only the head and shoulders of the person concerned shall be included in the photograph and the head from the chin to the top of the hair shall not be smaller 22mm and not larger
                                                                                than 25mm
Head Coverings:
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
- The person concerned shall be photographed without any head-gear or veil, and any other additions to the face which tend to alter the natural likeness shall be removed. (The Director-General
                                                                                may exempt any person or any category of persons from any of these requirements.)
Glasses and Head Covers
If you wear glasses:
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses.
- No dark tinted lenses or heavy frames
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Spectacles may be worn provided that the lenses do not make the eyes invisible. Spectacles with dark lenses may be worn if the eyes will appear distorted without them or if the
                                                                                person is blind
Expression:
The Photograph must: 
- Show you looking at the Camera with a neutral expression and mouth closed
- No obvious grinning, frowning or raised eyebrows.
- The face shall be a recognizable likeness of the person and the head, hair, eyebrows, eyes, nose and moustache or beard, if any, shall be sharply and clearly defined
- Neither the photograph nor the copies thereof may be defaced by holes, pencil or ink marks or in any other manner
Please refer Photo specification guide (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2IclBw38KZwQVjy2ocWbbh/6f49b8129c7de56eea9802bfbd2c6873/photo_specification.pdf)

## Processing Time
 Applicants should take into consideration that the process of examining a fully completed application is 10 working days..
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

## Application Form
Please download and complete fully the application form below, ensure it is signed and please bring with you when attending for your appointment. 
Visa Application form – (BI84) Form 11 (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3cgxyx7zO6y0TUvTpBHQng/d1c33011fbddbd98146eac0485c74170/APPLICATION-FOR-VISA-OR-TRANSIT-VISA.pdf)
Download Instructions:

OR

- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
